items.headline: Mauritius weather bulletin – 31 March 2026
items.summary: Weather news for Mauritius issued at 16h30 on Tuesday 31 March 2026.General situation:Clouds coming from the Northeast will influence the local weather tonight.Forecast for the next 24 hours:Partly cloudy tonight and early tomorrow morning with showers to the North, East and over the highgrounds.The minimum temperature will be between 19 and 21 degrees Celsius over the central plateau and between 23 and 25 degrees Celsius along the coasts.Cloud developments are expected during the day to the South, Southwest and over the Central Plateau with localised showers.These showers will be moderate and thundery.The public is advised to:1. remain in safe places and avoid open areas, hiking, sea ventures and sheltering under trees in case of thundery showers.2. be cautious on the roads due to reduced visibility resulting from showers and fog patches.The maximum temperature will be between 26 and 29 degrees Celsius over the highgrounds and between 30 and 33 degrees Celsius over the coastal regions.The wind will blow from the Northeast at about 15 km/h, becoming variable at times.Sea moderate beyond the reefs with Southwesterly swells of the order of 2 metres.High tides :Tomorrow 00h52 and 12h48.Low tides :18h48 and tomorrow 07h10.Sunset :18h11.Sunrise (tomorrow) :06h17.Atmospheric pressure at 16h00:1011 hectopascals.

items.headline: Judge temporarily halts construction of Trump's White House ballroom
items.summary: The Republican-appointed judge ruled that Trump was the "steward" of the White House, but not the "owner".

items.headline: Peacekeepers killed by roadside explosion in Lebanon, initial report finds
items.summary: The deaths of the two Indonesian nationals came less than a day after another UN peacekeeper - also Indonesian - was killed in Lebanon.

items.headline: Lebanon condemns Israel’s 'clear intention' to impose new occupation of its territory
items.summary: Lebanon said Israeli strikes hit near Beirut’s airport road on Tuesday as the UN Security Council met after three peacekeepers were killed. Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz said troops would occupy parts of southern Lebanon post-war, prompting Beirut to warn of a “clear intention” to impose a new occupation.

items.headline: Tiger Woods pleads not guilty to DUI in car crash where opioids found
items.summary: Plea comes hours after sheriff's report said the golfer showed signs of impairment at the scene of last week's crash.

items.headline: Bosnia and Herzegovina dump Italy out of World Cup 2026 qualifier
items.summary: After the match was level at 1-1 after extra time, Bosnia and Herzegovina beat Italy 4-1 on penalties to reach finals.

items.headline: US Supreme Court rules against ban on 'conversion therapy' for LGBTQ minors
items.summary:
[France 24] The US Supreme Court ruled Tuesday against a Colorado state law banning "conversion therapy" for LGBTQ minors, siding with a Christian therapist who challenged it on the grounds of free speech. At issue is the constitutionality of a 2019 Colorado law that prohibits licensed practitioners from conducting "conversion therapy" on patients under 18. The Guardian Washington DC bureau chief David Smith reports.

[Al Jazeera] In an eight-to-one decision, the high court ruled against a law banning the discredited practice on free speech grounds.

items.headline: Russian oil tanker docks in Cuba ending near-total blockade
items.summary:
[BBC News] A Russian oil tanker has docked in Cuba, providing temporary relief after months of shortages.

[Al Jazeera] Russian tanker delivers enough fuel to meet Cuba’s energy needs for up to 10 days, following a three-month blockade.

items.headline: ‘I need to leave’: Cuba’s tourism workers reel from US oil blockade
items.summary: From taxi drivers to tour guides, the collapse of Cuba’s tourism industry has left thousands struggling to get by.

items.headline: FBI agents who investigated Trump file lawsuit alleging retaliatory firing
items.summary: Former agents say that they were fired for work on probe into Trump's efforts to stay in power after 2020 election loss.

items.headline: EU faces 'largest oil supply disruption in history', energy expert says
items.summary: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, Francesco Sassi, Assistant Professor at the University of Oslo and energy geopolitics expert, says that the Iran war has provoked "the largest oil supply disruption in history" adding that "what is really concerning is that national governments will start to unilaterally take action, subsidising energy consumption or act to secure energy supplies, and this will inevitably increase the cost of natural gas and oil in Europe".

items.headline: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
items.summary:
[BBC News] The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

[France 24] A woman US journalist who was kidnapped in Iraq's capital city by a suspected Iranian-backed Iraqi armed group, US officials said on Tuesday. Advocacy groups identified the missing woman as Shelly Kittleson, a freelance journalist based in Rome who has covered several wars in the Middle East..

items.headline: US fuel prices surge past $4 a gallon
items.summary: The average price of regular gasoline in the United States topped $4 dollars a gallon for the first time since 2022, as the Iran war continues to disrupt global energy supply. This as inflation in the Eurozone rose past the ECB's target to 2.5% in March. Meanwhile, Egyptian shops and restaurants are having to close early due to the government's energy-saving measures.

items.headline: Turkiye and Sweden book World Cup 2026 spots as Kosovo and Poland bow out
items.summary: Turkiye beat Kosovo 1-0, and Sweden beat Poland 3-2, as winners secure latest FIFA World Cup 2026 qualifying spots.

items.headline: King's state visit to US to go ahead in April, but no Harry meeting
items.summary: Despite political tensions between the US and UK, the King will travel to Washington next month.

items.headline: As Ukraine commemorates the Bucha massacre, Russia rejects idea of Easter truce
items.summary:
[France 24] Ukraine is commemorating the fourth anniversary of the Bucha massacre, named after a town who many of its residents are thought to have been massacred by Russian invading troops in the early weeks of the war. Since, the conflict hasn't slowed down, as Russia keeps pounding the country. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle tells us more.

[Al Jazeera] Ukraine's president hosts EU leaders to mark Bucha massacre anniversary, says negotiator Umerov in Turkiye for talks.

items.headline: Eurovision Song Contest launches first-ever Asia edition
items.summary:
[BBC News] Broadcasters from 10 countries, including South Korea and the Philippines, will be taking part.

[France 24] The Eurovision Song Contest Asia 2026 has confirmed artists from at least 10 countries across Asia competing: Thailand, South Korea, the Philippines, Vietnam, Malaysia, Cambodia, Laos, Bangladesh, Nepal and Bhutan. More are expected to join before the finale in November. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis reports.

items.headline: Report finds there are no safe places for women in Darfur
items.summary: In tonight's edition, Doctors Without Borders sounds the alarm over Sudan, warning sexual violence against women and girls is being used as a weapon of war. Also, a Tunisian ⁠court handed down a ​two-year prison sentence to news website editor Ghassen Ben Khelifa in ​the latest prosecution targeting media workers. And after stops in New York, London and Melbourne, the Africa Fashion exhibition arrives in Paris.

items.headline: Eritrea advance after 19-year absence from Afcon qualifying
items.summary: Eritrea are one of six nations to reach the group stage of qualification for Afcon 2027, having played their first games in the competition since 2007.

items.headline: Five EU countries 'deliberately eroding' rule of law, report shows
items.summary: A new report by the Civil Liberties Union of Europe points to “deliberate erosion of the rule of law” in Bulgaria, Croatia, Hungary, Italy, and Slovakia. FRANCE 24's Brussels correspondent Dave Keating reports.

items.headline: Italian Christmas meal tragedy turns into murder inquiry
items.summary: Tests results suggest the two women may have been killed with ricin, according to Italian media, but police still do not have a suspect.

items.headline: Hungarian FM says reports he passed information to Russia are 'foreign interference'
items.summary: Hungarian Foreign Minister Peter Szijjarto said on Tuesday that reports from Eastern European media outlets that he passed "strategic information" to Russia were "foreign interference". In one leaked phone call Szijjarto is alleged to have told Russian counterpart Sergei Lavrov that he was "at his service".

items.headline: Hiring in the US drops to pandemic lows as job market under Trump stagnates
items.summary: Labour market stagnation mirrors a slowing private payroll growth, averaging just 18,000 monthly in recent months

items.headline: Analysis: Hezbollah backs Iran ambassador as conflict deepens Lebanon rifts
items.summary: Lebanon’s expulsion of Iranian ambassador tests limits of state power as Hezbollah fights push for disarmament.

items.headline: Gulf allies privately make the case to Trump to keep fighting until Iran is decisively defeated
items.summary: Gulf states, led by Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates, are urging Trump to continue prosecuting the war against Iran, arguing that Tehran hasn't been weakened enough by the bombing campaign, according to U.S., Gulf and Israeli officials. FRANCE 24's Mark Owen speaks with Dr H. A. Hellyer, Senior Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute. He stresses that, despite some Gulf States pressing to prolong the war, none of the Gulf States want the Iranian state to collapse.

items.headline: Burundi's main city rocked by blasts after electrical fire at military store
items.summary: Multiple blasts hit Burundi's main city of Bujumbura on Tuesday after an electrical fire broke out at a military arsenal in the east African nation. An army spokesperson said emergency services were intervening and urged the public to remain calm after the incident sparked fears of a coup against the government.

items.headline: Brandy honored with star on Hollywood Walk of Fame
items.summary: Singer and actor Brandy was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame Monday

items.headline: Iran’s FM confirms contact with US envoy Witkoff, denies talks under way
items.summary: Iran's top diplomat Araghchi has told Al Jazeera that, despite contacts, Iran is not in negotiations with the US.

items.headline: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
items.summary:
[BBC News] Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

[France 24] Israel's defence minister has vowed his army will block residents from returning to southern Lebanon after the war and will be destroying all homes in villages along the border, in which he implied would be a Gaza-like scale of destruction as seen in Rafah and Beit Hanoun. More than 1200 people have been killed in Lebanon and over 1.2 million displaced since Israel began its offensive against Hezbollah at the start of March.

[France 24] The Lebanese have seen it all, from civil war to the 2019 protests demanding an end to revolving door politics, from the 2020 port of Beirut explosion that exposed the tragic consequences of nepotism to Israel’s 2024 operation to neutralize longtime foe Hezbollah. Now Israel’s ground forces are back to again take on Tehran-backed foe Hezbollah. Thus, Lebanon finds itself the second front of the month-long war launched against Iran.

items.headline: Europe pushes back on US military operations as concerns over Iran war mount
items.summary: France and Italy have joined Spain in pushing back on U.S.-Israeli military operations, sources said on Tuesday, as U.S. President Donald Trump singled out NATO allies in Europe as unhelpful in the month-long war, highlighting divisions. The decisions came against a backdrop of tensions between Washington and key partners over the war in Iran. ​Earlier this month, Trump called ‌longtime NATO allies "cowards" over their lack of support against Iran. FRANCE 24's Eliza Herbert reports.

items.headline: US-Israel launch major attacks as Iranian authorities maintain defiance
items.summary: Bunker-buster bombs trigger massive secondary explosions at a mountainous area near Isfahan overnight.

items.headline: One month of war on Iran cost Arab countries up to $194bn: UNDP
items.summary: Even if conflict is 'short but intense', countries like Sudan, Yemen, Lebanon will see major increases in poverty rates.

items.headline: Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks face death penalty under new Israeli law
items.summary:
[BBC News] The new law, passed on Monday, was pushed hard by the far-right and Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir.

[Africanews] Israel’s parliament on Monday passed a law approving the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis, a measure that has been harshly condemned by the international community and rights groups as discriminatory and inhumane.

items.headline: Biens saisis : la FCC annonce une vente aux enchères publique en avril

items.summary: Iraq's Ministry of Interior says one suspect arrested after 'unknown individuals' kidnap journalist.

items.headline: South African politician takes dip in pothole to show city management failures

À l’issue de la judicial review entendue le 13 mars 2026, la Cour a statué que la suppression du droit de résidence des Chagossiens était illégale et a ordonné son annulation. items.headline: South Africa launches major security operation to tackle crime and gang violence
items.summary: In a show of force in Cape Town, around 500 military and police personnel paraded ahead of a year-long deployment targeting gangs, illegal mining, and organized crime across the Western Cape.

items.headline: EU shows support to Ukraine at Bucha commemoration
items.summary: Four years on from the Bucha massacre, the Ukrainian town is remembering its dead. Russian military forces retreated from Bucha in 2022, hundreds of bodies were discovered, including women, children and elderly people, many of the corpses had been left lying in the streets. A delegation of EU foreign ministers arrived in Kyiv on March 31 to mark the anniversary and discuss ways to further support Ukraine.

items.headline: Senegal doubles prison sentence length for same-sex relations
items.summary: Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye has signed into law legislation doubling the maximum penalty for same-sex relations, making them punishable by up to 10 years in prison amid a crackdown on the country's gay community.

items.headline: At least 16 killed and thousands displaced by gang attack in rural Haiti
items.summary:
[BBC News] The attack happened in the Artibonite region, where members of the Gran Grif gang have targeted locals in the past.

[Africanews] Gran Grif attacked Petite-Rivière de l’Artibonite early Sunday, burning homes and leaving bodies strewn on the streets. The gang attacked again on Monday, said Bertide Horace, spokesperson for the Commission for Dialogue, Reconciliation and Awareness to Save the Artibonite, an activist group

items.headline: Céline Dion's triumphant return to the city of lights: Ten Paris concerts confirmed
items.summary:
[France 24] The rumours turned out to be true: Céline Dion will return to the stage in the French capital in September and October at the La Défense Arena. As fans gear up to get their tickets, FRANCE 24's Olivia Salazar-Winspear takes a closer look at the Canadian superstar's love affair with Paris.

[Africanews] Under the lights of the Eiffel Tower, French-Canadian superstar Céline Dion has shared a powerful comeback announcement. Under the lights of the Eiffel Tower, French-Canadian superstar Céline Dion has shared a powerful comeback announcement.

items.summary: Solomon Ogbonna Eziko's installation as an Igbo leader in East London has sparked tensions.

items.headline: [Ki Zistwar Zordi] 31 mars — La tour Eiffel inaugurée devient une icône mondiale
items.summary: Inaugurée le 31 mars 1889 à l’occasion de l’Exposition universelle de Paris, la tour Eiffel s’est rapidement imposée comme l’un des symboles les plus emblématiques de la France et du génie architectural de son époque. Conçue par l’ingénieur Gustave Eiffel, la structure métallique, haute de plus de 300 mètres, avait initialement suscité de vives critiques de la part de certains artistes et intellectuels, qui la jugeaient inesthétique. Malgré ces réticences, elle a attiré dès ses premiers jours de

items.headline: King Charles III to make state visit to the US in April despite calls to cancel over Iran war
items.summary: The UK's King Charles III will visit the US and Bermuda in April, Buckingham Palace announced on Tuesday, as critics called for the trip to be cancelled due to concerns over the US's ongoing war with Iran.

items.headline: International Booker Prize 2026 shortlist announced featuring French witch, sworn virgin
items.summary: The organisers of the International Booker Prize announced on Tuesday the 2026 shortlist for the prestigious literary award which recognises works of fiction from around the world that have been translated into English.The six finalists include French novelist and playwright Marie NDiaye, winner of the country's top Goncourt prize.

items.headline: One Coach’s fight against drugs for Kano’s youth
items.summary: On a dusty field in Kano, coach Hida Ghaddar is doing far more than running football drills. Through her Breakthrough Soccer Academy, she offers young people an alternative to the growing risks around them, combining sport with discipline, education, and job support.

items.headline: Premature babies evacuated from Gaza strip return to their parents
items.summary: A group of toddlers evacuated as premature infants just a month into the Israel-Hamas war returned Monday to families overjoyed yet worried about their futures in a transformed Gaza.

items.headline: Santé : le programme de vaccination contre le PVH étendu aux écoles primaires
items.summary: Le programme de vaccination contre le papillomavirus humain (PVH, ou HPV en anglais) a été étendu aux écoles primaires de l’île Maurice, avec une augmentation significative de la couverture au début de l’année 2026. Selon les données du ministère de la Santé, les campagnes ont été menées dans 32 écoles en janvier, puis dans 76 établissements en février, illustrant l’intensification de la stratégie de vaccination en milieu scolaire. Au total, 3 384 élèves ont été vaccinés au cours de cette périod

items.headline: [Tech-à-tête] Lenovo Legion Go Fold redéfinit le gaming portable avec un concept pliable
items.summary: Lenovo a présenté au Mobile World Congress (MWC) 2026 un concept innovant baptisé Legion Go Fold, un appareil hybride combinant console portable, tablette et mini ordinateur portable. Ce prototype se distingue par son écran POLED pliable, capable de passer de 7,7 pouces en mode compact à 11,6 pouces une fois déployé, offrant ainsi une expérience de jeu plus immersive tout en conservant une bonne portabilité. Pensé comme un appareil polyvalent, le Legion Go Fold propose plusieurs modes d’utilisat

items.summary: Le Premier ministre Navin Ramgoolam a fermement rejeté l’existence d’un prétendu « Gang de Cinq » lors de la tranche du PNQ, ce mardi, au Parlement. « Il n’y a pas de « Gang de Cinq ». Ce sont des personnes que je connais et qui viennent me voir », a-t-il déclaré. items.headline: Niger coup leader Bazoum still detained as presidential term ends
items.summary: The presidential term of Niger’s ousted leader, Mohamed Bazoum, officially ends on Thursday, but he is unlikely to be released from detention or even put on trial, leaving the situation uncertain.

Parmi ces cas, plusieurs concernent des récidivistes, notamment 46 cas de récidive (deuxième infraction), 5 cas pour une troisième infraction et 2 cas pour une quatrième infraction. items.headline: La SBM enregistre des bénéfices de Rs 4,2 milliards
items.summary:
[Defimedia] Le Groupe SBM a publié ses résultats pour la période close au 31décembre 2025. Le bénéfice après impôts se chiffre à Rs

[ION News] Le Groupe SBM a réalisé un bénéfice après impôts de Rs 4,2 milliards pour l’exercice financier 2025, confirmant une performance solide dans un contexte économique marqué par des incertitudes internationales. Les produits d’exploitation du groupe ont progressé de 14,4 % pour atteindre Rs 19 milliards, soutenus par la croissance des activités principales. items.headline: Oil price steadies for now but cost of petrol soars around the world
items.summary: The price of a barrel of oil has steadied with signs the US is open to ending the war against Iran, even if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. But at the petrol pump, consumers are still paying the price for the global supply chain disruption.

items.headline: Silver Bank : Fin du conservatorship, un receiver nommé
items.summary:
[L'Express (via mega.mu)] La *Bank of Mauritius* a mis fin, ce 30 mars 2026, au conservatorship de Silver Bank Limited et nommé un receiver, Huns Biltoo de KPMG Mauritius, pour piloter la suite du dossier, faute de reprise concrétisée. Placée sous administration provisoire depuis le 13 février 2024 en vertu de la *Banking Act 2004*, la banque n’a pas réussi à finaliser son processus de rachat. Plusieurs investisseurs s’étaient manifestés, mais un seul avait soumis une demande formelle pour acquérir une ...

[News Moris] The Bank of Mauritius has terminated the conservatorship of Silver Bank Limited and moved the institution into receivership, effective immediately. The decision, announced on Monday, 30 March 2026, follows a comprehensive review of the bank’s financial standing and available … The post Silver Bank Enters Receivership on 30 March 2026 After Failed Sale Process appeared first on News Moris.

items.summary: Face aux défis du retour des talents mauriciens expatriés, le gouvernement s’apprête à revoir en profondeur son dispositif d’incitation. items.summary: Il se croyait insaisissable. Pendant des semaines, Jean Erwin Onézime, 27 ans, sans domicile fixe, a semé la terreur dans plusieurs quartiers de la région nord de Port-Louis, multipliant les vols, les agressions et les cambriolages avant d'être finalement rattrapé par la justice. C'est à Karo Kalyptis que les équipes du CID et du DCIU Metro North ont mis la main au collet de ce suspect particulièrement difficile à localiser. items.headline: Dubreuil : Entre sérénité et défis de transport
items.summary: À Dubreuil, village au charme préservé et aux habitants chaleureux, la vie s’écoule avec sérénité, rythmée par les traditions et la culture du thé. Ce tableau paisible ne cache cependant pas les zones d’ombre : transports publics irréguliers, infrastructures inachevées et services locaux à améliorer. Quelles que soient leur culture ou religion, les habitants de Dubreuil sont unanimes à dire que la vie y est calme et marquée par une vraie connexion entre eux. Ce village a co ...

items.headline: Soupçons de négligence à l’hôpital SAJ : Un jeune de 26 ans décède, sa famille remet en question la prise en charge médicale
items.summary: Un drame inexpliqué à l’hôpital SAJ, à Flacq, a plongé une famille dans une douleur et une détresse indescriptibles. C’est l’histoire d’un jeune de 26 ans, plein d’espoir et de projets, dont la vie s’est brutalement arrêtée dans des circonstances que son père qualifie aujourd’hui de *«troublantes»*. items.summary: Une affaire de violences domestiques a éclaté à Brisée-Verdière, impliquant une femme de 35 ans, soupçonnée d’avoir agressé verbalement puis physiquement sa belle-mère, âgée de 66 ans, dans un contexte marqué par la consommation d’alcool. items.summary: Le chanteur, auteur et compositeur Joseph Nicholas Emilien est actuellement hospitalisé. items.headline: Rivière-du-Rempart : Entre inquiétude et réorganisation pour les marchands de légumes

items.headline: 30 mars 2013–30 mars 2026 : Le souvenir intact d’une tragédie qui hante toujours
items.summary: Dans un silence chargé d’émotion, la ville de Port-Louis s’est arrêtée, hier, pour honorer la mémoire des 11 victimes des inondations meurtrières du 30 mars 2013. Devant la stèle érigée à proximité de l’entrée du tunnel du Caudan, familles endeuillées, proches, amis, mais aussi ministres, élus et simples citoyens se sont recueillis, le temps de quelques minutes suspendues, unis dans le souvenir de ceux que les eaux ont emporté. items.headline: Météo : Pluie et orages en vue

items.headline: Incendie au Victoria Urban Terminal: Quatre étals détruits

items.headline: Uber fait son entrée à Maurice, avec un modèle adapté
items.summary: La question de la présence d’Uber à Maurice a longtemps suscité des interrogations. Si la plateforme de transport est aujourd’hui évoquée sur l’île, son déploiement reste particulier et différent de celui observé dans d’autres pays.

items.headline: À Triolet: Vol d’équipements audio d’environ Rs 275 000 dans une voiture
items.summary: Un vol d’équipements audio a été signalé à 7ème-Mille, Triolet, impliquant une voiture garée dans une cour privée. Les faits se seraient produits entre le 25 et le 26 mars, à une heure indéterminée, selon les premières informations recueillies.

items.headline: Household gas: the price of a 12kg cylinder increases from Rs190 to Rs250
items.summary: The Council of Ministers has approved an upward revision of the price of a 12 kg household gas cylinder, which will increase from Rs190 to Rs250.

items.headline: Trafic routier : 752 000 véhicules circulent à Maurice à février dernier
items.summary: Maurice comptait presque 747 000 véhicules à fin décembre dernier. Selon le bureau national des statistiques, une hausse de 5% a été enregistrée en 2025. Et d’après les derniers chiffres disponibles au National Land Transport Authority, presque 5 200 nouvelles unités ont été acquises en janvier et février.

items.headline: Algeria's former president Liamine Zeroual laid to rest with military honours
items.summary: There were full military honors for Algeria’s former president, Liamine Zeroual, on Monday as the 84 year-old former general was laid to rest in his home town of Batna.

items.headline: Mondial 2026 : face à la menace des cartels, le Mexique renforce sa sécurité
items.summary: À quelques mois du coup d’envoi du Mondial de football, le Mexique fait la une des journaux en raison de la capacité de ces organisations criminelles à terroriser la population. La mort du baron de la drogue El Mencho, tué le 22 février par l’armée, a provoqué une flambée de violence dans tout le pays. Mais la présidente Claudia Sheinbaum l’assure : les conditions de sécurité seront réunies pour accueillir les spectateurs et les équipes. Gianni Infantino, le président de la FIFA, s’est également

items.headline: Le Real Madrid se prépare à encaisser l’argent d’une opération à la Luis Suárez
items.summary: Almería n’a pas encore réussi à retrouver la Liga, mais le club andalou sait rentabiliser ses saisons en deuxième division espagnole. La raison : les performances de ses buteurs. La saison dernière, Almería a terminé sixième du classement, mais Luis Suárez lui a permis de décrocher le jackpot l’été dernier. items.headline: Kenya voter registration drive targets young people ahead of 2027 general election
items.summary: In 2024, Kenya’s so-called Gen-Z protests against government corruption and rights abuses swept the country. But since then, many young people have disengaged. Now a new voter registration drive is hoping to get them back on board.

items.headline: Mauritius Faces Strict Water Curbs as 7 Reservoirs Hit 66.7% Capacity
items.summary: Mauritius faces the prospect of rigorous new water usage curbs within days as a persistent rainfall deficit leaves the island’s primary reservoirs at just two-thirds capacity.
